title: Loss of Nuclear TDP-43 Is Associated with Decondensation of LINE Retrotransposons, ATAC-Seq

name: Loss of Nuclear TDP-43 Is Associated with Decondensation of LINE Retrotransposons (ATAC-Seq)

wiki_pages.markdown:
**Summary:** Loss of the nuclear RNA binding protein TAR DNA binding protein-43 (TDP-43) into cytoplasmic aggregates is the strongest correlate to neurodegeneration in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis and frontotemporal degeneration. The molecular changes associated with the loss of nuclear TDP-43 in human tissues are not entirely known. Using a novel subcellular fractionation and fluorescent activated cell sorting method to enrich for diseased neuronal nuclei without TDP-43 from post-mortem FTD-ALS human brain, we characterized the effects of TDP-43 loss on the transcriptome and chromatin accessibility. Nuclear TDP-43 loss is associated with gene expression changes that affect RNA processing, nucleocytoplasmic transport, histone processing and DNA damage. Loss of nuclear TDP-43 was also associated with chromatin decondensation around long interspersed nuclear elements (LINEs) and increased LINE1 DNA content. Moreover, loss of TDP-43 leads to increased retrotransposition that can be inhibited with antiretroviral drugs, suggesting that TDP-43 neuropathology is associated with altered chromatin structure including decondensation of LINEs. **Overall Design:** Examination of gene expression profiles and chromatin accessibility in neuronal nuclei, which have either retained or lossed TDP-43, extracted from FTD/ALS patient post-mortem frontal cortex **Contact:** EdwardB Lee, Perelman School of Medicine at the University of Pennsylvania **Contributors:** ElaineY Liu, Jenny Russ, ChristopherP Cali, JessicaM Phan, Alexandre Amlie-Wolf, EdwardB Lee **Publications:** - ***Loss of Nuclear TDP-43 Is Associated with Decondensation of LINE Retrotransposons*** Cell Reports 2019 Apr 30 [10.1016/j.celrep.2019.04.003](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.celrep.2019.04.003) [PMID:31042469](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31042469) - ***TDP-43 represses cryptic exon inclusion in the FTD-ALS gene UNC13A*** Nature 2022 Mar [10.1038/s41586-022-04424-7](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-022-04424-7) [PMID:35197626](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35197626)
**Related Datasets:** This dataset is part of GEO SuperSeriesGSE126543 which includes the following additional datasets - [**Loss of Nuclear TDP-43 Is Associated with Decondensation of LINE Retrotransposons (RNA-seq-Seq)**](https://ampals.synapse.org/Explore/Datasets/DetailsPage?id=67754663)
